refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect. The refined FR explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated), incorporates the safety and policy constraints as boundary conditions, and defines the evaluation context (representative operations, agreed period). This makes the requirement testable and traceable to stakeholder needs (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) via onboard instrumentation and/or independent survey. Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via incident log and safety audit. Confirm operational policy adherence via task logs and supervisor review.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks with sufficient speed, precision, and load capacity to achieve ≥ productivity_target output rate.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator control with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve ≥ productivity_target output rate when operating remotely.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (m³/h or ton/h) to the Site Manager and operator for task monitoring and optimization.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with autonomous task planning and real-time productivity monitoring, coupled with a low-latency teleoperation overlay and safety interlock system that enforces worksite policies.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with configurable attachments, operator-in-the-loop task execution, and a safety-critical middleware layer that mediates all commands against worksite policies before transmission to vehicle actuators.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid architecture: vehicle executes pre-planned task sequences with adaptive real-time adjustments based on operator input (on-site or remote), with a safety services layer that monitors task execution and halts operations if policy violations are detected.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid architecture: vehicle executes pre-planned task sequences with adaptive real-time adjustments based on operator input (on-site or remote), with a safety services layer that monitors task execution and halts operations if policy violations are detected.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle task execution and teleoperation control: mitigated by explicit operator input channels (on-site joystick, remote command protocol) that both feed into a unified task sequencer.

Coupling between safety services and productivity: mitigated by designing safety constraints as hard boundaries (e.g., geofence, speed limits) rather than soft penalties; safety enforcement is orthogonal to task execution efficiency.
Coupling between telemetry reporting and real-time control: mitigated by separating telemetry pipeline (asynchronous, best-effort) from control loop (synchronous, safety-critical).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits low coupling because it cleanly separates concerns: (1) vehicle dynamics and task execution, (2) operator input (dual-mode), (3) safety policy enforcement, and (4) telemetry/monitoring. Each concern maps to a distinct subsystem with well-defined interfaces.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Operator (on-site or remote) can reliably perceive task state and provide corrective input within the latency budget of the vehicle control loop (assumed ≤ 500 ms for on-site, ≤ 2 s for remote).
ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety policies can be formalized as computable constraints (e.g., geofence, speed limits, no-dig zones) that the safety services layer can enforce without ambiguity.
RISK: If teleoperation latency exceeds 2 s, remote operator situational awareness degrades, reducing effective productivity and increasing collision risk.
RISK: If safety policy constraints are overly restrictive or poorly tuned, they may artificially cap productivity below target even when operations are safe.
RISK: Hybrid execution (pre-planned + adaptive) requires robust synchronization between task sequencer and operator input; desynchronization could cause task failures or safety violations.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) latency budgets for on-site and remote control loops, (2) a formal safety policy specification language (e.g., constraint templates), (3) a task sequencer design that guarantees atomic transitions between pre-planned and operator-adjusted states, and (4) a fallback mode (e.g., safe stop) if synchronization is lost.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that encompasses all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their orchestration. At this level, the IM assignment reflects that FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is a system-level acceptance criterion that cannot be localized to a single subsystem; it requires coordinated delivery across vehicle performance, teleoperation capability, and safety policy enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h), vehicle state (position, orientation, load), operator mode (on-site vs. remote), safety status. Consumed by Site Manager dashboard and operator HMI.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task definitions (target location, attachment type, sequence), operator commands (on-site joystick or remote command protocol), worksite policy updates. Sourced from Site Manager, operator, and safety manager.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Policy violation warnings, geofence breaches, collision alerts, emergency stop signals. Consumed by operator, tele-operator, and safety manager.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for teleoperation (command/telemetry), safety services (policy updates, alerts), and productivity monitoring. Connects on-site equipment, remote tele-operator station, and cloud/edge safety services.

module_containment_scores.notes: FR-NGHE-001 is explicitly a system-level acceptance criterion. All sub-functions (vehicle task execution, teleoperation, safety enforcement, telemetry) are contained within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle subsystem delivers core productivity (task execution speed, precision, load capacity) but cannot independently achieve FR-NGHE-001 without teleoperation capability and safety services. Productivity measurement requires telemetry integration (diffused to NGHE Solution level). Operator input (on-site or remote) is external to Vehicle scope.

Teleoperation enables remote operator control but does not directly execute tasks or measure productivity. Productivity depends on vehicle performance and safety constraints. Teleoperation is a capability enabler, not a containment boundary for the productivity KPI.

Safety Services enforces policy constraints but does not generate productivity. Safety constraints are boundary conditions on vehicle execution, not the primary driver of productivity. Productivity measurement and task execution are diffused to Vehicle and NGHE Solution levels.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: FR-NGHE-001 is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires coordinated delivery across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. No single subsystem can be assigned ownership of this FR; instead, the NGHE Solution integration boundary is responsible for ensuring that the combined subsystems achieve the productivity target while maintaining safety and policy compliance. Ownership is assigned to the System Integration Team, which orchestrates vehicle performance, teleoperation capability, and safety policy enforcement.
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle subsystem underperforms (e.g., slow cycle time, low load capacity), productivity target cannot be achieved regardless of teleoperation or safety services quality. Mitigation: establish vehicle performance baselines early in design; validate against productivity target in representative conditions.

RISK: If Teleoperation introduces excessive latency or fidelity loss, remote operator productivity degrades. Mitigation: specify latency budgets (≤ 2 s) and fidelity requirements (e.g., video frame rate ≥ 30 fps) in teleoperation DP.

RISK: If Safety Services constraints are overly restrictive, they artificially cap productivity. Mitigation: involve Safety Manager in policy tuning; validate that safety constraints do not degrade productivity below target in representative operations.

RISK: Diffusion of productivity measurement across multiple subsystems (vehicle sensors, telemetry pipeline, monitoring dashboard) increases risk of measurement error or inconsistency. Mitigation: establish a single source of truth for productivity metrics; validate measurement methodology with Site Manager.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution (system-level) and decompose into sub-FRs for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Each sub-FR should specify its contribution to the overall productivity target (e.g., Vehicle: ≥ 95% of target, Teleoperation: ≥ 90% of target when remote, Safety Services: ≤ 5% productivity penalty). This makes ownership and accountability explicit at each subsystem level while maintaining traceability to the system-level acceptance criterion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid architecture with pre-planned task sequences, adaptive operator input, and safety policy enforcement) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 by enabling task execution with operator control (on-site or remote) and safety constraints. The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide subsystem design (Vehicle task sequencer, Teleoperation control interface, Safety Services policy layer) and is testable via representative operations trial. No independence violations detected: the DP does not over-constrain the design space (e.g., it does not mandate specific hydraulic components or teleoperation protocols).

diffusion_risks: Productivity measurement is diffused across Vehicle (task execution sensors), Telemetry interface (data pipeline), and NGHE Solution (aggregation and reporting). Mitigation: establish a single telemetry schema and validation protocol.

Operator input (on-site joystick vs. remote command) is diffused across Vehicle (on-site input hardware) and Teleoperation (remote command protocol). Mitigation: unify input handling in a task sequencer module that accepts both input modes.

Safety policy enforcement is diffused across Safety Services (policy evaluation) and Vehicle (actuator-level enforcement). Mitigation: define a clear safety-critical interface between Safety Services and Vehicle (e.g., SafetyAlert output triggers vehicle safe-stop).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it encompasses all subsystems and their orchestration. The four external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimize diffusion. Subsystem boundaries (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and do not leak across the system boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-NGHE-001 is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period, confirm compliance with safety plan and policies, and validate that productivity ≥ target. Verification requires instrumentation (onboard sensors, telemetry pipeline, independent survey) and stakeholder sign-off (Site Manager, Safety Manager). Feasibility is high if subsystem performance baselines are established early.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution (system-level). Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote control capability), Safety Services (policy enforcement), and Telemetry (productivity monitoring). Allocation is clear and traceable. Ownership is assigned to System Integration Team with support from subsystem teams.

recommended_revisions: Establish quantitative productivity baselines for each subsystem (Vehicle: ≥ 95% of target, Teleoperation: ≥ 90% when remote, Safety Services: ≤ 5% penalty) to enable early validation and risk mitigation.
Define latency budgets for on-site (≤ 500 ms) and remote (≤ 2 s) control loops to ensure operator situational awareness and task execution fidelity.
Formalize worksite safety policies as computable constraints (geofence, speed limits, no-dig zones) to enable unambiguous enforcement by Safety Services.
Establish a single source of truth for productivity metrics (e.g., vehicle odometer + load sensor) to minimize measurement error and ensure consistency across subsystems.
Plan representative operations trial early (e.g., Phase 2 of development) to validate productivity target achievement and identify design gaps before production release.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into subsystem-level FRs (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry). The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass the FR-DP-IM gates, but further decomposition is necessary to allocate productivity responsibility to subsystems and enable detailed design. Proceeding directly to detailed design without subsystem-level FRs would risk diffusion of ownership and accountability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall execute earthmoving tasks with cycle time, precision, and load capacity such that task output ≥ 95% of productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) under representative operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency ≤ 2 s, video fidelity ≥ 30 fps, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve ≥ 90% of productivity_target when operating remotely.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies with ≤ 5% productivity penalty (i.e., safety constraints reduce achievable output by no more than 5% relative to unconstrained vehicle performance).
FR-NGHE-001.4: The Telemetry subsystem shall measure and report real-time productivity (m³/h or ton/h) with ≤ 5% measurement error and ≤ 1 s latency to enable operator and Site Manager monitoring.

assumptions: Operator (on-site or remote) can reliably perceive task state and provide corrective input within latency budget.
Worksite safety policies can be formalized as computable constraints (geofence, speed limits, no-dig zones).
Vehicle performance baselines (cycle time, precision, load capacity) are achievable and validated in representative conditions.
Teleoperation latency budget (≤ 2 s for remote) is acceptable to customer and does not degrade productivity below target.
Safety policy constraints can be tuned to achieve ≤ 5% productivity penalty without compromising safety.
Productivity measurement methodology (sensors, telemetry pipeline, aggregation) is validated and agreed with Site Manager.
open_risks: Vehicle performance may not meet 95% productivity baseline if hydraulic system or attachment design is suboptimal. Mitigation: early performance validation in representative conditions.
Teleoperation latency may exceed 2 s if network infrastructure is inadequate. Mitigation: conduct network latency survey at customer site; specify network requirements in system specification.
Safety policy constraints may be overly restrictive, capping productivity below target. Mitigation: involve Safety Manager in policy tuning; validate in representative operations.

Productivity measurement error may exceed 5% if sensors are poorly calibrated or telemetry pipeline is unreliable. Mitigation: establish measurement validation protocol; cross-check with independent survey.

Diffusion of productivity responsibility across subsystems may lead to accountability gaps. Mitigation: establish clear ownership and success criteria for each subsystem; conduct regular integration reviews.
evidence_needed: Vehicle performance baseline (cycle time, precision, load capacity) validated in representative conditions.
Teleoperation latency and fidelity measurements from customer site network.
Formalized worksite safety policies (geofence, speed limits, no-dig zones) from Safety Manager.
Productivity measurement methodology (sensors, telemetry pipeline, aggregation) validated with Site Manager.
Representative operations trial results (≥ 40 operating hours) demonstrating productivity ≥ target with safety and policy compliance.
Subsystem-level performance baselines (Vehicle: ≥ 95%, Teleoperation: ≥ 90%, Safety Services: ≤ 5% penalty, Telemetry: ≤ 5% error).
